company.what_they_do: punkproof.com is a public email service provider — it offers free email addresses to the public under its domain. It is not a company building a product or service in the traditional B2B/SaaS sense. The site explicitly positions itself as a legitimate (non-throwaway) public email host, emphasizing spam prevention, prohibition of illegal activity, and abuse reporting infrastructure.
company.raw_notes: Homepage copy describes a public free email service. Key claims: (1) primary goal is spam prevention and safe/secure environment, (2) prohibits illegal activities, (3) does not allow sending messages to the internet, (4) explicitly states it is NOT a throwaway/disposable email service and that emails will not bounce, (5) asks receivers not to block their domain, (6) suggests banning problematic users by IP rather than domain, (7) has an abuse ticket system. This pattern — public inbound-only email with anti-abuse messaging directed at third-party site operators — is characteristic of a shared public email domain used by many individuals, not a single organization's corporate domain.
